contract_scope: Human-readable descriptor attached to a service registered in Semantic DNS. The descriptor text (name + description + capabilities + tags) is embedded once at register time and stored as a vector keyed svec-{hex}; at query time POST /api/discover embeds the query and ranks registered services by brute-force cosine similarity against these vectors (#S-1, #S-2, #S-5).
honesty_rules: The descriptor is additive to king_semantic_dns_register_service. It does not replace the keyword/attribute path; services without a descriptor are still discoverable by the existing keyword criteria.
service_id is the same identity key used by king_semantic_dns_register_service. One descriptor per service_id.
service_type mirrors the Semantic-DNS service_type the descriptor belongs to (e.g. king.inference.v1); required for scoped discovery.

name + description + capabilities[] + tags[] are concatenated into a single embedding input by model_inference_service_descriptor_embedding_text(). That text is what gets embedded; the individual fields are only retained for display and for the BM25 token path in #S-6.
capabilities[] and tags[] are free-form strings. They are not enums; the demo does not constrain domain vocabulary.
Descriptor content is assumed non-sensitive. Embedding and storage paths do not redact it.

service_id.note: must match service_id used in king_semantic_dns_register_service

rejection_codes.invalid_service_descriptor: any required field missing, out of range, or of the wrong type; also unknown top-level keys
